title: China Arrival Payment and Internet Checklist
description: A build-time JSON example showing how ChinaTripBox can publish static pages directly from data_pipeline records.
summary: This page is generated from data_pipeline/pages/arrival-payment-internet-checklist.json at Astro build time.
Use the same structure for generated guides, city facts, tool landing pages, and other static data products.

sections.heading: Before departure
body: Install Alipay, WeChat, your eSIM app, your airline app, and an offline translation app before you fly.
Keep a small RMB cash backup plan because wallet verification, bank fraud checks, and roaming problems can all happen during the first day.
bullets: Verify your payment wallet before leaving home.
Save your hotel address in English and Chinese.
Download offline maps or screenshots for the airport-to-hotel route.

sections.heading: After landing
body: Turn on your data option first, then test payment with a low-stakes purchase before relying on QR payment for taxis, metro tickets, or restaurants.
If your wallet fails, use a staffed counter, hotel front desk, or ATM instead of repeatedly trying the same blocked card.

faqs.question: Is this JSON page rendered dynamically on the server?
faqs.answer: No. Astro reads the JSON during the build and writes a normal static HTML page into dist.

faqs.question: Can data_pipeline generate many pages this way?
faqs.answer: Yes. Add more JSON files under data_pipeline/pages and Astro will include them in getStaticPaths during the next build.
